text.title: SQL Optimization Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the SQL Optimization methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Every optimisation MUST start with `EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS, FORMAT TEXT)` capturing the current plan; index/rewrite changes without a captured baseline plan are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without a baseline plan, the planner's actual choice is invisible; optimisation guesses produce indexes that are never used.

rule.statement: When actual rows differ from estimated by >10x, the report MUST run `ANALYZE` on the table and re-capture the plan; ignoring statistics skew is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Planner chooses join order + index use on estimates; skewed estimates produce wrong plans that no new index fixes.

rule.statement: Composite indexes MUST place equality columns first, range columns last; reversed ordering is rejected unless a documented reason applies.
rule.rationale: B-tree uses prefix columns for equality + traverses range on last; reversed ordering makes the index unusable for equality.

rule.statement: Pagination beyond 1k rows MUST use keyset (cursor) pagination keyed on `(last_seen_at, id)`; OFFSET-based pagination at depth is forbidden.
rule.rationale: OFFSET scans and discards rows on every page; cursor uses an index seek regardless of depth.

rule.statement: Subqueries in `SELECT` lists MUST be rewritten as JOIN + aggregation; per-row subqueries create N+1 patterns.
rule.rationale: A subquery in SELECT executes once per outer row; JOIN executes once regardless of row count.

rule.statement: Expensive aggregations queried frequently MUST be precomputed via materialized view with `REFRESH CONCURRENTLY`; re-computing on every read is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Re-computing GROUP BY over millions of rows on every dashboard load is wasteful; MV + CONCURRENTLY refresh avoids the read lock.

rule.statement: Optimisation PR MUST include before/after EXPLAIN + wall-clock delta + sample size; un-evidenced 'looks faster' claims are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without evidence, regressions ship; explicit deltas with sample sizes pass review.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
